Teamfight Tactics Tier List

This Teamfight Tactics champions tier list ranks all 64 champions by win rate for Set 17, sorted from S-tier down to F-tier so you can read the current meta at a glance. The S-tier — the 7 strongest champions right now — features Apex Primordian (97.6%), Jhin (66.1%), Shen (65.3%), Blitzcrank (62.8%), and Bard (61.9%). Apex Primordian posts the single highest win rate at 97.6%. For players hunting an edge, Apex Primordian is the most underrated pick this patch — S-tier results at only a 0.1% pick rate — while Illaoi looks overrated, popular at 18.8% but landing in F-tier. Rankings update daily from real ranked matches, so every placement reflects current performance rather than a one-off result. Use the tiers below to pick the best champions for your games.

S

S Tier - Meta Defining

(7)

S-tier is led by Apex Primordian, which dominates the meta with a 97.6% win rate on a 0.1% pick rate. They are followed by Jhin (66.1%), Shen (65.3%), Blitzcrank (62.8%), Bard (61.9%), Nami (59.1%), and Morgana (59.1%). These champions define the current meta and are the safest, highest-impact picks. In terms of popularity, Blitzcrank is the crowd favourite here at a 22.7% pick rate, while Apex Primordian is slept on at just 0.1% — a strong S-tier pick flying under the radar.

A

A Tier - Strong Picks

(6)

A-tier is led by Kindred, which dominates the meta with a 59.0% win rate on a 14.3% pick rate. They are followed by Sona (59.0%), Fiora (58.3%), Rhaast (57.7%), Teemo (55.2%), and The Mighty Mech (55.2%). These are strong, reliable champions that perform well in almost every game. In terms of popularity, Rhaast is the crowd favourite here at a 23.1% pick rate, while Sona is slept on at just 10.8% — a strong A-tier pick flying under the radar.

B

B Tier - Viable

(10)

B-tier is led by Samira, which dominates the meta with a 55.0% win rate on a 14.0% pick rate. They are followed by Karma (54.9%), Riven (54.9%), Gwen (54.7%), Ornn (54.2%), Vex (54.1%), Rammus (53.7%), and Nasus (53.4%). The remaining 2 champions in this tier average a 52.9% win rate. These champions are balanced and fully viable with solid execution. In terms of popularity, Karma is the crowd favourite here at a 21.4% pick rate, while Vex is slept on at just 8.2% — a strong B-tier pick flying under the radar.

C

C Tier - Situational

(9)

C-tier is led by Bel'Veth, which performs strongly with a 52.3% win rate on a 17.8% pick rate. They are followed by Maokai (52.3%), Tahm Kench (52.2%), Xayah (52.0%), Master Yi (51.8%), Urgot (51.4%), Aatrox (51.2%), and Milio (50.8%). The remaining 1 champions in this tier average a 50.5% win rate. These champions are situational and need favourable matchups or team comps to shine. Despite the low ranking, Maokai stays popular at a 29.5% pick rate, meaning players overvalue it relative to its C-tier results, while Master Yi (8.8%) is more rightly avoided.

D

D Tier - Below Average

(10)

D-tier is led by Mordekaiser, which holds steady with a 50.1% win rate on a 26.3% pick rate. They are followed by Gnar (50.0%), Nunu & Willump (50.0%), Graves (49.9%), Akali (49.7%), Aurelion Sol (49.3%), Kai'Sa (49.3%), and Jax (49.2%). The remaining 2 champions in this tier average a 49.2% win rate. These champions underperform against the current meta and take extra effort to win with. Despite the low ranking, Mordekaiser stays popular at a 26.3% pick rate, meaning players overvalue it relative to its D-tier results, while Zed (1.0%) is more rightly avoided.

F Tier - Weak

(22)

F-tier is led by Fizz, which struggles with a 48.9% win rate on a 12.2% pick rate. They are followed by Gragas (48.1%), Pantheon (48.1%), Cho'Gath (48.0%), Caitlyn (47.8%), LeBlanc (47.6%), Lulu (47.5%), and Lissandra (47.5%). The remaining 14 champions in this tier average a 44.0% win rate. These champions are the weakest options this patch and are best avoided in ranked. Despite the low ranking, Illaoi stays popular at a 18.8% pick rate, meaning players overvalue it relative to its F-tier results, while Zoe (3.9%) is more rightly avoided.

TFT Set 17 Champions FAQ

What are the best TFT champions in Set 17?

The best champions in Set 17 are: Apex Primordian (97.6% WR), Jhin (66.1% WR), Shen (65.3% WR), Blitzcrank (62.8% WR), Bard (61.9% WR). These S-tier units provide the strongest value and highest win rates.

How are TFT champion tiers determined?

Champion tiers are based on win rate, pick rate, and average placement. S-tier champions consistently place higher and win more games. Our algorithm analyzes millions of matches to provide accurate rankings.

Which champion has the highest win rate in Set 17?

Apex Primordian has the highest win rate at 97.6% in Set 17. Building around this champion gives you a strong chance at top 4.

Should I always play S-tier champions?

While S-tier champions have the best stats, TFT success depends on flexibility. If S-tier units are contested, pivoting to A or B-tier alternatives can be more effective. This tier list helps identify options at each tier level.

How often is the TFT tier list updated?

Our TFT tier list is updated frequently with fresh data from ranked matches. The data reflects current champion performance and any balance changes.
